Online</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">online.stanford.edu <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› courses  › soe-yeeqmse01-quantum-mechanics-scientists-and-engineers</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers | Course | Stanford Online\">Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers | Course | Stanford Online</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">March 17, 2020 -</span><!--]--> <!---->The text Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers (Cambridge, 2008) is recommended for the course, though it is not required. It follows essentially the same syllabus, has additional problems and exercises, allows you to go into greater depth on some ideas, and also contains many additional topics for further study.<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[5--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--> svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"favicon-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><!--[0--><div class=\"favicon-background-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><img class=\"favicon-background svelte-on1hvy\" loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">Stanford Online</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">online.stanford.edu <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› courses  › soe-yeeqmse-02-quantum-mechanics-scientists-and-engineers-2</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 2 | Course | Stanford Online\">Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 2 | Course | Stanford Online</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">August 21, 2024 -</span><!--]--> <!---->This course covers key topics in the use of quantum mechanics in many modern applications in science and technology, introduces core advanced concepts such as spin, identical particles, the quantum mechanics of light, the basics of quantum information, and the interpretation of quantum mechanics, and covers the major ways in which quantum mechanics is written and used in modern practice.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://online.stanford.edu/courses/soe-yeeqmse-02-quantum-mechanics-scientists-and-engineers-2\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" tabindex=\"-1\"><!--[0--><img src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/uhU4o8yjlyG1-D5i9hpeGx_R-BzG2UEdTH2SYGWXuSY/rs:fit:200:200:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cHM6Ly9vbmxp/bmUuc3RhbmZvcmQu/ZWR1L3NpdGVzL2Rl/ZmF1bHQvZmlsZXMv/c3R5bGVzL2VtYmVk/ZGVkX2xhcmdlL3B1/YmxpYy8yMDE4LTA5/L3F1YW50dW0tbWVj/aGFuaWNzLWZvci1z/Y2llbnRpc3RzLWVu/Z2luZWVyc19TT0Ut/WUVFUU1TRTAxLmpw/Zz9pdG9rPS1HTHpO/UTVv\" <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----></a><!----><!--]--><!----></div><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--><!--]--><!--[5--><!--[0--><div class=\"snippet  svelte-jmfu5f\" data-pos=\"4\" data-type=\"web\" data-keynav=\"true\"><div class=\"result-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><div class=\"result-content svelte-1rq4ngz\"><a href=\"https://www.classcentral.com/course/stanford-openedx-quantum-mechanics-for-scientists-and-engineers-2-3057\" target=\"_self\" class=\"svelte-14r20fy l1\"><div class=\"site-name-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"favicon-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><!--[0--><div class=\"favicon-background-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><img class=\"favicon-background svelte-on1hvy\" src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/Pt-T3EujBlJkqiSNuZ1AR3QNtWaAQ-TW_i9X6JD6FMY/rs:fit:32:32:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cDovL2Zhdmlj/b25zLnNlYXJjaC5i/cmF2ZS5jb20vaWNv/bnMvY2ZjNjJlNDE2/OThhYTVjN2E4ZTIw/ZjlmNjNlMTdiZGZk/ZjMxNWQ3OWEyMjQ0/MTQ0NDlhNjVhMjEx/MjQ0NDM2MC93d3cu/Y2xhc3NjZW50cmFs/LmNvbS8\" alt=\"\" loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" aria-hidden=\"true\"/></div><!--]--> <img alt=\"🌐\" class=\"favicon svelte-w2a9kc size-s\" src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/Pt-T3EujBlJkqiSNuZ1AR3QNtWaAQ-TW_i9X6JD6FMY/rs:fit:32:32:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cDovL2Zhdmlj/b25zLnNlYXJjaC5i/cmF2ZS5jb20vaWNv/bnMvY2ZjNjJlNDE2/OThhYTVjN2E4ZTIw/ZjlmNjNlMTdiZGZk/ZjMxNWQ3OWEyMjQ0/MTQ0NDlhNjVhMjEx/MjQ0NDM2MC93d3cu/Y2xhc3NjZW50cmFs/LmNvbS8\" loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">Class Central</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">classcentral.com <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">  › subjects  › science  › physics  › quantum mechanics</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Free Course: Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 2 from Stanford University | Class Central\">Free Course: Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 2 from Stanford University | Class Central</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->The course is designed to build on a first course on quantum mechanics at the junior or senior college level, so students should have at least that background. The material here is specifically matched to follow on from the Stanford Online QMSE-01 &quot;Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers&quot; class, and all the material from that class is provided as background in the online course materials here.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://www.classcentral.com/course/stanford-openedx-quantum-mechanics-for-scientists-and-engineers-2-3057\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" tabindex=\"-1\"><!--[0--><img src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/TiG4AqHpCOApnu5bJoYcmZTjxoInaFZ_ZaZe0jYeKOA/rs:fit:200:200:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cHM6Ly9jY3dl/Yi5pbWdpeC5uZXQv/aHR0cHMlM0ElMkYl/MkZ3d3cuY2xhc3Nj/ZW50cmFsLmNvbSUy/RmltYWdlcyUyRm1l/dGElMkZtZXRhLWRl/ZmF1bHQucG5nP2F1/dG89Zm9ybWF0JmFt/cDtpeGxpYj1waHAt/NC4xLjAmYW1wO3M9/YTk4MGIzYjdiNWM4/M2ExNWVhYzZkYTQ5/YmJiYmMxODI\" alt=\"\" width=\"112\" height=\"112\" loading=\"lazy\" class=\"svelte-1yrspal\" onload=\"this.__e=event\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----></a><!----><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://www.classcentral.com/course/stanford-openedx-quantum-mechanics-for-scientists-and-engineers-2-3057\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 1 from Stanford University | Class Central\">Free Course: Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 1 from Stanford University | Class Central</div></a><!----> <!--[4--><div class=\"product-review desktop-default-regular svelte-dj4i6e\"><strong class=\"line-clamp-1\">Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 1</strong> <!--[0--><div class=\"line-clamp-2\"><!---->Explore <strong>quantum</strong> <strong>mechanics</strong> fundamentals and applications in engineering and science, from wave equations to the hydrogen atom, with a focus on practical problem-solving techniques.<!----></div><!--]--> <div class=\"attributes desktop-small-regular svelte-dj4i6e\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--> src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/lVbYDi4R10eYmFNrJ7BjvIUzLK4V_VqXyGYRT3l1n8Y/rs:fit:32:32:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cDovL2Zhdmlj/b25zLnNlYXJjaC5i/cmF2ZS5jb20vaWNv/bnMvNmIzYWVlM2Zk/ZWI2NzA2ZGE5Zjk0/MWI2OGNlZTcwYzM2/ZjQwNDA4MzAyMDcy/YTgxYzhkMzNjODY5/OTJlMTgwYy9hcmNo/aXZlZC1idWxsZXRp/bi5zdGFuZm9yZC5t/b2JpLw\" loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">Stanford ROTC</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">archived-bulletin.stanford.mobi <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› schoolofhumanitiesandsciences  › physics</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Physics | Stanford University\">Physics | Stanford University</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->We will discuss paradigms for harnessing entanglement to solve hitherto intractable computational problems or to push the precision of sensors to their fundamental quantum mechanical limits. We will also examine challenges that physicists and engineers are tackling in the laboratory today to enable the quantum technologies of the future. PHYSICS 15. Stars and Planets in a Habitable Universe.<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--> <!--[--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--><!--[5--><!--[0--><div class=\"snippet  svelte-jmfu5f\" data-pos=\"11\" data-type=\"web\" data-keynav=\"true\"><div class=\"result-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><div class=\"result-content svelte-1rq4ngz\"><a href=\"https://theoreticalminimum.com/\" target=\"_self\" class=\"svelte-14r20fy l1\"><div class=\"site-name-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"favicon-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><!--[0--><div class=\"favicon-background-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><img class=\"favicon-background svelte-on1hvy\" src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/GQQnJptbP80XJFqmh5KlnY57t9VZKgisDhqndDGmG-g/rs:fit:32:32:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cDovL2Zhdmlj/b25zLnNlYXJjaC5i/cmF2ZS5jb20vaWNv/bnMvNjBkM2Q2YTg2/MGJmZDY1NWRmYmY3/YjE1MTJkNjRjMDY3/YzZiNWJkMTMwZjYz/M2RlMDI1ZTY3N2Y3/ODljYmIyZC90aGVv/cmV0aWNhbG1pbmlt/dW0uY29tLw\" src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/GQQnJptbP80XJFqmh5KlnY57t9VZKgisDhqndDGmG-g/rs:fit:32:32:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cDovL2Zhdmlj/b25zLnNlYXJjaC5i/cmF2ZS5jb20vaWNv/bnMvNjBkM2Q2YTg2/MGJmZDY1NWRmYmY3/YjE1MTJkNjRjMDY3/YzZiNWJkMTMwZjYz/M2RlMDI1ZTY3N2Y3/ODljYmIyZC90aGVv/cmV0aWNhbG1pbmlt/dW0uY29tLw\" loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">The Theoretical Minimum</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">theoreticalminimum.com <span class=\"text-ellipsis\"></span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"The Theoretical Minimum |\">The Theoretical Minimum |</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->The Theoretical Minimum is a series of Stanford Continuing Studies courses taught by world renowned physicist Leonard Susskind. These courses collectively teach everything required to gain a basic understanding of each area of modern physics including all of the fundamental mathematics. The sequence begins with the modern formulations of classical mechanics discovered by Lagrange and Hamilton in the late 18th and 19th centuries, and then moves on to the radical new theories of relativity and quantum mechanics discovered by Albert Einstein and others in the early 20th century.<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--><!--]--><!--[5--><!--[0--><div class=\"snippet  svelte-jmfu5f\" data-pos=\"12\" data-type=\"web\" data-keynav=\"true\"><div class=\"result-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><div class=\"result-content svelte-1rq4ngz\"><a href=\"https://www.cambridge.org/highereducation/books/quantum-mechanics-for-scientists-and-engineers/D7E998F63F840E09A1E98E6FF33694A5\" target=\"_self\" class=\"svelte-14r20fy l1\"><div class=\"site-name-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"favicon-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><!--[0--><div class=\"favicon-background-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><img class=\"favicon-background svelte-on1hvy\" loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">Cambridge Aspire website</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">cambridge.org <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› highereducation  › books  › quantum-mechanics-for-scientists-and-engineers  › D7E998F63F840E09A1E98E6FF33694A5</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers | Cambridge Aspire website\">Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers | Cambridge Aspire website</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">June 5, 2012 -</span><!--]--> <!---->David A. B. Miller, Stanford University, California ... If you need a book that relates the core principles of quantum mechanics to modern applications in engineering, physics, and nanotechnology, this is it. Students will appreciate the book&#x27;s applied emphasis, which illustrates theoretical concepts with examples of nanostructured materials, optics, and semiconductor devices.<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--><!--]--><!--[5--><!--[0--><div class=\"snippet  svelte-jmfu5f\" data-pos=\"13\" data-type=\"web\" data-keynav=\"true\"><div class=\"result-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><div text-ellipsis\">Eastonlee</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">eastonlee.com <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› blog  › 2017  › 05  › 24  › quantum-mechanics-for-scientists-and-engineers-by-stanford-university</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers by Stanford University\">Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers by Stanford University</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">May 24, 2017 -</span><!--]--> <!---->Months earlier, I searched open courses of Quantum Physics, it turned out Coursera had no good courses, so I chose an looking-ok course by Stanford Online. Why did I choose Quantum Physics, because it was developed relatively late and is rather mysterious to me. Of course I will dabble in other physics branches in the future, but I’m happy with Quantum as my first step. ... Quantum Mechanics requires some prerequisites, most of which I’ve mastered, several disciplines need relearning to keep fresh in my mind.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://eastonlee.com/blog/2017/05/24/quantum-mechanics-for-scientists-and-engineers-by-stanford-university/\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--><!--]--><!--[5--><!--[0--><div class=\"snippet  svelte-jmfu5f\" data-pos=\"14\" data-type=\"web\" data-keynav=\"true\"><div class=\"result-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><div class=\"result-content svelte-1rq4ngz\"><a href=\"https://online.stanford.edu/courses/cs259q-quantum-computing\" target=\"_self\" class=\"svelte-14r20fy l1\"><div class=\"site-name-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"favicon-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><!--[0--><div class=\"favicon-background-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><img class=\"favicon-background svelte-on1hvy\" loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">Stanford Online</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">online.stanford.edu <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› courses  › cs259q-quantum-computing</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Computing | Course | Stanford Online\">Quantum Computing | Course | Stanford Online</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">November 18, 2022 -</span><!--]--> <!---->This course introduces the basics of quantum computing.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://online.stanford.edu/courses/cs259q-quantum-computing\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" tabindex=\"-1\"><!--[0--><img src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/5JPAetUU2PVAJu6HIR-EnqzwFfSwCPwn1Xz2NEt1eSs/rs:fit:200:200:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cHM6Ly9vbmxp/bmUuc3RhbmZvcmQu/ZWR1L3NpdGVzL2Rl/ZmF1bHQvZmlsZXMv/MjAyMi0xMS8lMjBR/dWFudHVtJTIwQ29t/cHV0aW5nX0NTMjU5/US5qcGc\" alt=\"\" width=\"112\" height=\"112\" loading=\"lazy\" class=\"svelte-1yrspal\" onload=\"this.__e=event\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----></a><!----><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[5--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://online.stanford.edu/courses/cs259q-quantum-computing\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" tabindex=\"-1\"><!--[0--><img
